subject: Mathematics (NCERT - Ganita Prakash)
chapter: Another Peek Beyond the Point (Decimals - Multiplication and Division)

activities_projects.title: The 10-Times Jump
materials: A small pebble (as a decimal point)
steps: Place a number card like '45.6' on the table with a pebble as the decimal
Multiply by 10 by jumping the pebble one place to the right ($456$)
Multiply by 100 by jumping two places, adding a zero if needed ($4560$)
Record the 'new' numbers in your notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes decimal point shifts during multiplication by powers of 10
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct placement of the decimal point (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Grocery Bill Multiplier
activities_projects.objective: Practice basic multiplication of decimals by whole numbers
materials: Empty snack packets with price tags
steps: Pick a packet with a price like ₹9.50
Calculate the cost of 5 such packets using addition ($9.5 + 9.5...$)
Calculate the same using multiplication ($9.5 	imes 5$)
Check if both answers match
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects repeated addition to decimal multiplication
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the multiplication result (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Left-Shift Division
steps: Write a large number like '123.0'
Divide by 10 by moving the decimal one step to the left ($12.3$)
Divide by 100 by moving it two steps ($1.23$)
Create a 'pattern rule' for division based on the number of zeros
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters the rule for dividing decimals by 10, 100, and 1000
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct direction of decimal movement (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Money Matters: Sharing is Caring
activities_projects.objective: Divide decimals representing money by whole numbers
steps: Imagine you have ₹75.50 to share equally among 5 friends
Divide 7550 paise by 5 first
Convert the result back to rupees and paise using a decimal point
Verify by multiplying the answer by 5
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies decimal division to real-life sharing scenarios
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct division and decimal placement (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Decimals to Fractions Bridge
activities_projects.objective: Multiply decimals by converting them to fractions
steps: Take the problem $0.2 	imes 0.3$
Write them as fractions: $\frac{2}{10} 	imes \frac{3}{10}$
Multiply the numerators ($6$) and denominators ($100$)
Convert the final fraction $\frac{6}{100}$ back to decimal ($0.06$)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understand the logic behind the number of decimal places in a product

activities_projects.title: Lengthy Estimates
steps: Measure the width of one finger in cm (e.g., 1.2 cm)
Multiply this by 10 to estimate the width of your palm
Measure your palm to see how close your 'decimal estimate' was
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses decimal multiplication for physical estimations
activities_projects.evaluation: Reasonable estimation and correct calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Zero Placeholder Game
steps: Solve $0.5 \div 10$ and $0.5 \div 100$
Observe how many zeros are added after the decimal point
Create a rule: 'When I move left and run out of digits, I add a ___'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Correctly uses zeros as placeholders in small decimals

activities_projects.title: Area of a Mini-Garden
activities_projects.objective: Apply multiplication to find the area of a rectangle
steps: Draw a rectangle with length 4.5 cm and width 2 cm
Multiply length $	imes$ width to find the area
Count the squares on the graph paper to verify your decimal answer
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects decimal multiplication to geometric area
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct area calculation with units ($cm^2$) (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Signpost Sorting
steps: Calculate $0.1 	imes 10$ and $0.1 	imes 0.1$
Compare: which result is greater than the original $0.1$ and which is smaller?
Discuss why multiplying by a decimal less than 1 makes the number smaller
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops a sense of scale for decimal products

activities_projects.title: The Decimal Dietitian
activities_projects.objective: Calculate total nutrients from decimal values
steps: Find 'Protein per 1g' (e.g., 0.12g)
Calculate the total protein in a 10g serving ($0.12 	imes 10$)
Represent the answer in a small chart
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses decimals to interpret real-world health data
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate calculation based on label data (10 marks)
